question.text: Which of the following restrictions applies to the Trustee of an InvIT?

answer.text: The Trustee can invest up to 10% in units of the InvIT
The Trustee shall not invest in units of the InvIT in which it is designated as the trustee
The Trustee must invest at least 1% in units of the InvIT
The Trustee cannot hold more than 5% of units of the InvIT

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The InvIT Regulations clearly state that the trustee of the InvIT shall not invest in units of the InvIT in which it is designated as the trustee. This is an absolute prohibition, not a percentage-based restriction. **The Trap:** The distractors suggest percentage-based limits, but the actual regulation is a complete prohibition on trustee investment in the InvIT's own units. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This prohibition ensures the trustee remains independent and free from conflicts of interest that could arise from being both a fiduciary and an investor.
